meta_title: Impact Doors in Davie Ranches, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Davie Ranches, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 140mph wind-rated, A+ BBB. Get a free quote today!
h1: Impact Doors Built for Davie Ranches Homes — Where Ranch-Style Living Meets Real Storm Protection
intro:
Davie Ranches is one of Broward County's most distinctive communities — a place where large lots, mature oak canopies, and the quiet proximity to Tree Tops Park create an environment that feels miles removed from the chaos of South Florida storm season. But the peacefulness of life along these shaded ranch-style streets doesn't insulate homeowners from the very real threats that come with living in a 140mph wind zone. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand that Davie Ranches is a community where homeowners take their properties seriously — and where the average home value of $520,000 means protecting your investment is not optional. It's essential.

Located approximately 12 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Davie Ranches sits outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) designation, but that distinction offers little comfort when a major hurricane makes landfall in South Florida. Florida Building Code still requires that all new door installations in this area meet rigorous hurricane-impact standards, and for good reason. The storms that have swept through Broward County — from the catastrophic winds of Hurricane Andrew in 1992 to the widespread destruction of Hurricane Wilma in 2005 and the prolonged battering of Hurricane Irma in 2017 — have demonstrated repeatedly that no inland community in this region is truly sheltered. With roughly 30% of homes in the area built before 1994, many properties are still running on standard builder-grade doors that were never designed to handle modern storm ratings.

Impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing replace that vulnerability with genuine, code-compliant security. Whether you're upgrading your primary entry door, replacing aging sliding glass doors that overlook a backyard oasis, or installing elegant French doors connecting your living space to an outdoor entertaining area, our ESW and CWI product lines deliver the performance Davie Ranches homeowners need. This isn't just about surviving the next storm — it's about protecting your family, reducing your insurance premiums, and adding long-term value to a home you've worked hard to build.

section_why.h2: Why Davie Ranches Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact door contractor in South Florida is not a decision to make lightly. There are plenty of companies operating across Broward County, but very few can match the depth of experience, the quality of installation, and the commitment to customer service that defines Premier Impact Windows & Roofing. With over 60 years of combined installation experience and more than 500 homes completed across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, we've built our reputation by doing the work right the first time — and by treating every homeowner the way we'd want our own family treated.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract our work. Every crew member who shows up at your Davie Ranches home is a trained Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee who has been vetted, certified, and held to our exacting standards. This matters enormously when it comes to impact door installation, where precision is everything. A door frame that isn't perfectly plumb, a threshold that isn't properly sealed, or a multi-point locking system that isn't correctly aligned can compromise the entire performance of your investment during a storm event. Our in-house teams eliminate those risks.

We're also deeply familiar with the specific permitting and inspection requirements administered through the Broward County Building Division — the office responsible for overseeing all residential construction and renovation in communities like Davie Ranches. Our team handles the permit application process on your behalf, coordinates with inspectors, and ensures that every installation is fully documented and code-compliant. For homeowners in ZIP codes 33330 and 33331, this kind of end-to-end service means no surprises, no delays, and no paperwork headaches.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing carries an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and is fully licensed and insured throughout the state of Florida. When you invite us into your home, you're working with a company that has earned trust the hard way — through consistent performance, honest communication, and an unwavering commitment to service excellence. That's what it means to work with the Kings of Service.

section_history.h2: Davie Ranches Has Faced the Storms — Here's What That History Means for Your Doors
section_history.body:
Broward County's storm history is not ancient history — it's a living record that continues to shape how smart homeowners think about protecting their properties. Davie Ranches and the surrounding communities of Davie, Cooper City, and Southwest Ranches have collectively weathered at least eight significant named storms since 1990, and the scars from several of those events are still visible in the way the region approaches building codes, insurance pricing, and home improvement priorities.

Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was the event that changed everything for South Florida. While Andrew's most catastrophic damage was concentrated in Miami-Dade County, its effects rippled northward through Broward with sustained winds and widespread structural failures that exposed just how unprepared the regional housing stock truly was. Then came Hurricane Wilma in 2005 — a storm that many Broward County residents still reference as their worst storm experience. Wilma moved unusually fast across the peninsula, generating powerful wind shear that caused extensive damage to entry doors, sliding glass doors, and garage doors throughout communities like Davie Ranches. Homes with standard builder-grade doors fared particularly poorly, with reported failures leading to full interior depressurization and catastrophic roof losses in some cases. Hurricane Irma in 2017, though less intense at landfall, brought prolonged high winds and debris loading that once again separated protected homes from vulnerable ones.

For the approximately 30% of Davie Ranches homes built before 1994 — before modern hurricane codes were fully adopted — this history carries an urgent message. Those original doors were not designed with today's wind load requirements in mind, and every storm season represents a renewed gamble. Upgrading to impact-rated doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the most direct way to close that gap and bring your home into alignment with the protection standards that Broward County now demands.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Broward County Requirements for Impact Doors in Davie Ranches
section_code.body:
Davie Ranches falls within Broward County's jurisdiction and is governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC), which establishes strict minimum standards for all hurricane-protection products installed in residential properties. While the community sits outside the formal High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation primarily covering Miami-Dade and Broward's coastal municipalities — the wind speed design requirements in ZIP codes 33330 and 33331 still mandate impact-resistant products rated for 140mph wind loads. This is not a technicality; it is a legally enforceable construction standard.

Under the Florida Building Code, all exterior doors installed in this wind zone must meet specific impact-resistance and large-missile impact testing standards as defined by the American Society of Testing and Materials (ASTM). Products must be tested and listed under protocols such as ASTM E1886 and ASTM E1996, which simulate both wind pressure and debris impact scenarios. Every ESW and CWI door that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs in Davie Ranches carries the appropriate product approvals and Notice of Acceptance (NOA) documentation required by the state and county.

All door replacement projects in Davie Ranches require a permit issued through the Broward County Building Division. This process involves plan submission, product approval verification, and post-installation inspection by a licensed county inspector. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire process on your behalf — from pulling the permit before work begins to scheduling the final inspection upon completion. Homeowners should be cautious of any contractor who suggests skipping the permit process; unpermitted work can void your homeowner's insurance coverage, create complications at the time of resale, and expose you to fines and mandatory removal orders. We do everything by the book, every time.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Engineered for Davie Ranches Conditions
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've built our product lineup around two brands that consistently deliver the performance, durability, and aesthetic versatility that Davie Ranches homeowners expect: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. These aren't budget brands selected to pad margins — they are professionally specified, code-compliant products that we install day in and day out across Broward County because they perform when it matters most.

ESW's impact door line is engineered specifically for the demanding wind load environment of South Florida. Their entry door systems feature heavy-gauge reinforced frames, multi-point locking mechanisms that engage the door frame at multiple contact points simultaneously, and laminated impact glass options that maintain their integrity even after absorbing direct debris strikes. For Davie Ranches homeowners who love the indoor-outdoor lifestyle that large lots and mature landscaping invite, ESW's sliding glass door systems offer expansive clear glass panels with the same structural protection — no more choosing between views and safety. CWI complements the ESW lineup with its own series of impact-rated French doors and decorative entry systems that are particularly well-suited to the architectural character of ranch-style and estate homes common throughout the community.

Both brands offer extensive customization options, including a variety of frame finishes, hardware selections, glass tints, and decorative glass designs that allow homeowners to upgrade their protection without compromising curb appeal. For a community where $520,000 average home values reflect a strong sense of pride in property presentation, this matters. Impact doors from ESW and CWI are not utilitarian add-ons — they are premium architectural elements that enhance the look of your home while delivering the structural performance your family's safety requires.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Davie Ranches — And Why the Investment Makes Sense
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions we hear from Davie Ranches homeowners is about cost — and it's a fair question. Impact door pricing in Broward County varies based on the size of the opening, the door style (entry, sliding glass, or French), frame material, glass package, and hardware selections. For a typical single impact entry door installation in a Davie Ranches home, homeowners can generally expect to invest in the range of $1,500 to $3,500 per unit installed. Sliding glass door systems and multi-panel French door configurations run higher, typically $3,000 to $7,000 depending on dimensions and specifications. Full-home door packages that include the primary entry, secondary entries, and sliding glass doors represent a larger investment but often unlock the most significant insurance savings and property value benefits.

For many homeowners, the upfront cost concern dissolves quickly when viewed through the lens of long-term return on investment. In a market where homes average $520,000 in value, impact doors are widely recognized as a premium upgrade that directly enhances resale value and buyer appeal. Real estate professionals in Broward County consistently report that impact-protected homes command stronger offers and spend less time on market than comparable non-protected properties. Beyond resale, the annual insurance premium savings — which we detail in the next section — can realistically offset a significant portion of your total investment over the life of the product.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing makes the path to protection more accessible through flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down available for qualified buyers. We work with a network of financing partners to structure terms that fit your budget — because we believe that the safety of your family and the protection of your home shouldn't be delayed by upfront cost barriers. Ask our team about current promotional financing offers when you request your free, no-obligation quote.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Davie Ranches Homeowners Who Install Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
Installing hurricane-impact-rated doors is one of the most effective actions a Broward County homeowner can take to reduce their annual homeowners insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has become increasingly volatile in recent years, with many carriers tightening underwriting requirements and raising base rates across South Florida — but impact-protected homes consistently earn meaningful discounts that help offset those broader trends. For Davie Ranches homeowners, the combination of impact doors and other wind mitigation features can produce annual premium savings of 15% to 45% depending on the carrier, the home's age and construction, and the scope of protection installed.

The mechanism for these savings is the Wind Mitigation Inspection Report — a formal assessment conducted by a licensed inspector that documents the hurricane-resistance features of your home and submits those findings to your insurance carrier for credit. When your inspector can verify that all openings, including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors, are protected by compliant impact-rated products, your home qualifies for the highest tier of wind mitigation credits available under Florida's insurance framework. Common carriers operating in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance, all recognize these credits, though the specific discount percentages vary by policy.

For homeowners in the 33330 and 33331 ZIP codes, where insurance costs on a $520,000 home can run $4,000 to $8,000 or more annually depending on the policy, even a 20% reduction represents $800 to $1,600 in savings every single year. Over a decade, that's a substantial contribution toward the total cost of your impact door investment. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can help connect you with a licensed wind mitigation inspector following your installation to ensure you capture every dollar of savings you've earned.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Davie Ranches if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While Davie Ranches sits about 12 miles from the Atlantic coastline and is not within the formal HVHZ designation, Broward County's wind zone requires impact-rated doors capable of withstanding 140mph winds. Storms like Wilma and Irma caused significant damage across inland Broward communities, and Florida Building Code requires impact-compliant products on all new door installations in this area regardless of coastal proximity.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Davie Ranches, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door pricing in Davie Ranches typically ranges from $1,500 to $3,500 for a standard entry door installation and $3,000 to $7,000 for sliding glass or French door systems, depending on size, style, and specifications. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified buyers. Contact us for a free, itemized quote tailored to your specific home and door configuration.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors in Davie Ranches, and who handles that process?
faqs.a: Yes, all impact door replacements in Davie Ranches require a permit issued through the Broward County Building Division. This involves product approval documentation, plan submission, and a final inspection by a county-licensed inspector. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from application to inspection scheduling — so you don't have to navigate the bureaucracy alone. We never suggest bypassing the permit process, as unpermitted work can void insurance coverage and complicate future home sales.

faqs.q: How long does it take to install impact doors in a Davie Ranches home?
faqs.a: The timeline for a typical impact door installation project in Davie Ranches involves two phases: permitting and installation. Permit approval through the Broward County Building Division generally takes 2 to 4 weeks. Once permits are in hand, the physical installation of most door projects — including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — can be completed in one to two days by our in-house crews. We keep you informed throughout the process and coordinate inspection scheduling so there are no unnecessary delays.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowners insurance by installing impact doors in Davie Ranches?
faqs.a: Davie Ranches homeowners who install qualifying impact doors can realistically save 15% to 45% on their annual wind insurance premiums, depending on their carrier and the full scope of wind mitigation features present in the home. A licensed wind mitigation inspector documents your upgrades in an official report submitted to your insurer for credit. On a $520,000 home with annual premiums of $5,000 or more, those savings can amount to $750 to $2,250 per year — a powerful return on your impact door investment.
